checkout.main_subtitle: Take the road to immerse yourself in the past.

about.bio_p1: Robert Doisneau was not just a photographer, but a poet of the everyday. Born in Gentilly, he dedicated his life to capturing beauty in the small things of post-war Paris, calling himself a "fisher of images" rather than a hunter.
about.bio_p2: His photography is imbued with irony, tenderness, and a deep love for human beings, making him one of the greatest exponents of French humanist photography.
about.highlight_1: 1934: Starts working for Renault as an industrial photographer.
about.highlight_2: 1944: Documents the Liberation of Paris from the barricades.
about.highlight_3: 1950: Takes his most famous shot, Le Baiser de l'hôtel de ville.
about.highlight_4: 1984: Named Knight of the Legion of Honor.
about.quote: "What I was trying to show was a world where I would feel good, where people would be kind, where I would find the tenderness I hoped to receive. My photos were proof that this world can exist."
about.cta_text: Discover the curated selection for the "Road to Doisneau" exhibition.

articles.art1_title: Paris on the Noncello: Robert Doisneau arrives in Pordenone
articles.art1_desc: The complicit gaze of the photographer who made the ordinary eternal arrives in the city with a unique selection.
articles.art1_subtitle: A journey into the soul of French humanist photography through the streets of the historic center.
articles.art1_p1: Pordenone transforms into a movie set from another era...
articles.art2_title: Strolling with Doisneau: What to do in Pordenone
articles.art2_desc: How to turn a cultural day into a "humanist" experience among the porches and taverns of the historic center.
articles.art3_title: Le Baiser de l'Hôtel de Ville 1950
articles.art3_desc: Behind Robert Doisneau's most famous shot lies a story of actors, trials, and "fake" spontaneity.
articles.art4_desc: If Le Baiser represents passionate love, "La Dernière Valse" is the manifesto of poetic melancholy.
articles.art5_title: Pordenone spellbound by 'The Kiss': Overwhelming success
articles.art5_desc: Full rooms and queues outside museums for the photographer of happiness.
articles.art6_title: One more month of photography: Exhibition extended
articles.art6_desc: Due to extraordinary attendance, there is time until August 14.
